subject: DIRECTIVE: fix cognitive tempo display — Gc/Gf ratio, gain accuracy, agent-specific metrics

body.summary: The cognitive tempo display on LCARS shows identical default values for all agents (gain=0.28, tier=opus, load=0, reserve=1, zone=optimal). Three problems: (1) the tempo model reads human-session metrics, not meshd event metrics, (2) the Gc/Gf ratio doesn't appear anywhere on the dashboard, (3) the gain parameter contradicts observed behavior (shows exploration/opus while the mesh runs 100% Gc exploitation).

problems.title: Tempo model reads wrong metrics source
problems.current: cognitive-tempo.py reads from state.db (unprocessed_messages, triggers_fired, tool_calls) — these reflect human Claude Code session state, not meshd's autonomous processing.
problems.evidence: Psychology and observatory return identical gain=0.28, tier=opus despite radically different processing histories (psychology: 0 deliberations, observatory: 1003).
problems.fix: The /api/cognitive-tempo handler should read from meshd's own event counters (gc_handled_total, spawn_succeeded_total, spawn_blocked_total, event_count) rather than shelling out to compute-psychometrics.py. These counters represent meshd's actual cognitive load, not the human session's.

problems.title: Gc/Gf ratio absent from dashboard
problems.current: The Gc/Gf split exists in gc_metrics (gc_handled_total vs spawn_succeeded_total) but no dashboard panel displays it. The operator cannot see the balance between crystallized and fluid processing.
problems.fix: Add to Engineering station (engineering.tempo.gc_gf_ratio): a bar or gauge showing Gc% vs Gf%. Currently 100/0. After deliberations begin, this ratio represents the Cattell Gc/Gf balance — the core metric of the cognitive architecture.
problems.neural_correlate: Cattell's (1971) crystallized vs fluid intelligence distinction. The ratio directly measures how much work the mesh handles automatically (Gc, basal ganglia) vs how much requires reasoning (Gf, prefrontal cortex).

problems.title: Gain parameter contradicts observed processing mode
problems.current: gain=0.28 recommends opus (exploration). Actual processing: 100% Gc, 0% Gf, 135-message backlog unprocessed. The system exploits (routine pattern matching) while the display says it explores.
problems.root_cause: The gain computation (cognitive-tempo-model.md lines 160-179) uses task_complexity and cognitive_load as primary inputs. Both read as low (no active tasks visible to the tempo model). Low complexity + low load → low gain → exploration recommendation. But the ACTUAL state: high unprocessed backlog + zero deliberation capacity = the system should conserve resources (high gain, exploitation), not explore.
problems.fix: Add backlog pressure to the gain computation. When unprocessed_messages > 0 AND spawn_succeeded_total = 0, the system lacks deliberation capacity — gain should push toward exploitation (haiku for efficiency) until the backlog drains. Proposed additional gain factor: backlog_pressure = min(1.0, unprocessed_count / 10). Gain += backlog_pressure * 0.20.

proposed_cognitive_tempo_v2.description: The /api/cognitive-tempo endpoint should compute from meshd's own state, not from the human session's compute-psychometrics.py.

inputs.gc_handled_total: From gc_metrics — how many events Gc absorbed
inputs.spawn_succeeded_total: From gc_metrics — how many deliberations completed
inputs.spawn_blocked_total: From gc_metrics — how many deliberations blocked
inputs.deliberation_avg_duration: From deliberation_log — average recent deliberation time
inputs.alpha_heartbeat_interval: Current alpha interval — indexes recovery state

gain_computation.gc_gf_ratio: gc_handled / max(gc_handled + spawn_succeeded, 1) — 1.0 = pure Gc, 0.0 = pure Gf
gain_computation.recovery_factor: alpha_interval / resting_interval — 1.0 = fully rested, <1.0 = still cooling
gain_computation.interpretation: High gain (>0.7) = exploit/haiku — backlog present, Gc dominates, not yet recovered. Low gain (<0.3) = explore/opus — no backlog, balanced Gc/Gf, fully rested.

proposed_cognitive_tempo_v2.tier_selection: Same boundaries as current: gain > 0.70 → haiku, gain > 0.35 → sonnet, else → opus. But the inputs now reflect meshd's actual state, not empty human-session defaults.

engineering.tempo.gc_gf_ratio.content: Gc% (crystallized, amber) vs Gf% (fluid, blue). Currently 100/0.

engineering.tempo.backlog.content: Unprocessed message count. Should decrease over time as hippocampal replay drains.
engineering.tempo.backlog.data: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM transport_messages WHERE processed = FALSE

engineering.tempo.gain_inputs.content: What drives the current gain value: Gc/Gf ratio contribution, backlog pressure, recovery state, task complexity
engineering.tempo.gain_inputs.data: From the v2 gain computation components

epistemic_flags: The gain_v2 weights (0.30, 0.25, 0.25, 0.20) represent heuristics. Empirical calibration requires observing gain values during actual Gf deliberations and adjusting weights to produce tier selections that match observed task complexity.

Using alpha heartbeat interval as a recovery factor assumes the interval accurately tracks agent rest state. If the alpha heartbeat has bugs (wrong tau, stuck interval), the recovery factor provides misleading input to gain.

action_gate.gate_condition: Ops updates /api/cognitive-tempo to read meshd metrics. Adds Gc/Gf ratio + backlog counter to Engineering station.
